title: ProviderItemEnvelope
description:
Provisional alpha envelope for a fetched provider item.

Wraps one item retrieved from a provider resource (an issue, file, message, document, commit, etc.) with provider-neutral metadata. The payload is screened content — it must not contain secrets, tokens, credentials, PII, or raw authentication material.

# Authority boundary

This envelope is a **provider fact**, not Bicameral evidence or a decision candidate. It does not carry:

- Local actor/session authority (`AuthorityContext`) - Review commands or signoff approval - Event-store write intent - Bot lifecycle authority (tracking state, compliance, governance verdicts) - `SourceBinding` approval or `Source` materialization

The item becomes Bicameral evidence only after explicit promotion through the existing ingestion and governance paths.

content.description: Screened textual content of the item. Must not contain secrets, tokens, credentials, or PII.

content_hash.description: Content hash (SHA-256) of the screened content. Downstream consumers verify if provided, compute if omitted.

fetched_at.description: When this item was fetched from the provider (ISO-8601).

freshness.description: Freshness metadata from the provider for this specific item.

provider.description: Provider system identifier (must match the descriptor's `provider`).

provider_metadata.description: Provider-supplied metadata that does not fit the typed fields above. Must not contain secrets, tokens, credentials, or PII.

resource_id.description: The resource this item belongs to (matches the descriptor's `resource_id`).

title.description: Human-readable title or subject line, if the item has one.

uri.description: Canonical URI for the item on the provider.

FreshnessMetadata.description: Provider-reported freshness metadata for a resource or item.

etag.description: Provider-reported entity tag or revision identifier.

item_count.description: Provider-reported item or version count, if available.
